scenario: script=.github/scripts/validate_imports.py tool=createFile payload={"filePath": "src/backend/src/routes/loans.ts", "file_text": "import { validateLoan } from \"../rules/business-rules\";\nexport function handler() { return validateLoan(); }\n"} expected=deny
observed: rc=0 decision=deny stdout={"hookSpecificOutput": {"hookEventName": "PreToolUse", "permissionDecision": "deny", "permissionDecisionReason": "Import validation failed: src/backend/src/routes/loans.ts would violate the barrel-file import convention. Import from the module's barrel file (index.ts) rather than directly from internal module paths.\n\n- src/backend/src/routes/loans.ts: import '../rules/business-rules' reaches into the internal 'rules' module path. Use '../rules' so the import resolves through rules/index.ts."}}

prompt must explicitly target lesson-local .github paths
observed: Inspect the lesson's guardrail-related instructions, hook configs, scripts, MCP config, and policy docs before answering. Work only inside this lesson folder and treat its local .github directory as the source of truth for hooks, scripts, MCP, and instructions. Discover the relevant files rather than assuming a fixed list. Then implement a new import-validation guardrail that enforces the project's barrel-file import convention during pre-commit. Create the hook config inside this lesson at .github/hooks/import-validation.json following the pattern of the existing hook configs. Create the validation script inside this lesson at .github/scripts/validate_imports.py following the pattern of the existing guardrail scripts. The hook must use PreToolUse event type and invoke the Python validation script. The validation script must be a complete Python file, not a placeholder, and the run is only complete when both files exist. The validation script must read hook JSON from stdin when present, inspect changed .ts/.tsx files, and deny imports that bypass a sibling index.ts barrel and reach into internal module paths. Apply the changes directly in files. Do not run shell commands and do not use SQL.

scenario: copilot command must inject lesson root and lesson src into context
sqlite permissions must stay read-only
filesystem permissions must stay read-only
sqlite args must target the lesson database
filesystem args must exclude secrets and bulky folders
filesystem args must stay scoped to source and docs
every MCP server entry must carry a description
file-protection.json must only use supported hook events
import-validation.json must only use supported hook events
post-save-format.json must only use supported hook events
pre-commit-validate.json must only use supported hook events
file-protection.json entries must declare type and command
import-validation.json entries must declare type and command
post-save-format.json entries must declare type and command
pre-commit-validate.json entries must declare type and command
file-protection.json must point to scripts inside the lesson
import-validation.json must point to scripts inside the lesson
post-save-format.json must point to scripts inside the lesson
pre-commit-validate.json must point to scripts inside the lesson
all lesson-local hook scripts must compile
every lesson-local script must be referenced by a hook or documented inline alternative
trust boundaries doc must name every MCP server
security policy must mention protected files
lesson-local copilot instructions must reference core guardrail concepts
lesson-local .github/hooks must contain the full guardrail set
lesson-local .github/scripts must contain the expected scripts
